Technology.name: Transformer
Technology.Summary: Attention-based neural architecture that underpins most modern large language models.

Technology.name: Mixture of Experts
Technology.Summary: Architecture that routes each input through a small subset of specialized sub-networks, enabling much larger total parameter counts at manageable inference cost.

Technology.name: Supervised Fine-Tuning
Technology.Summary: Training a pre-trained model on labelled input-output pairs to adapt it to a specific task or style.

Technology.name: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback
Technology.Summary: Training method that uses human preference data to shape a reward model, then optimizes the language model against it.

Technology.name: Direct Preference Optimization
Technology.Summary: An alternative to reward-model-based RLHF that learns directly from preference pairs without training a separate reward model.

Technology.name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
Technology.Summary: Pattern that retrieves relevant documents at query time and provides them as context to a language model, improving accuracy on knowledge-intensive tasks.

Technology.name: Speculative Decoding
Technology.Summary: Inference acceleration technique where a small draft model proposes tokens that a larger model then verifies in parallel.

Technology.name: Embedding
Technology.Summary: A dense vector representation of text, images, or other data that preserves semantic similarity under geometric operations.

Technology.name: Tokenizer
Technology.Summary: A component that splits raw text into discrete units (tokens) that a model can process, typically using byte-pair encoding or similar schemes.
